examples: Or, our carbonation-detecting skills could be an accident.
If that sounds odd, scientists have discovered that carbonation actually has a flavor.
Under the pressure of 650 feet (200 meters) of water, this carbon dioxide stays dissolved, much like the carbonation in a bottle of soda.
The carbonation and artificial sweeteners in soda can also lead to bloating, belching and excess flatulence.
On our 7-Up, you can almost taste the carbonation in them it's so good.
